# WEYLAND-YUTANI CRT — GRUB2 theme  ::  generated by Alien CRT Studio
# Color-only menu (no extra font/pixmap deps) for maximum compatibility.
# Profiled for Nobara. Real menu entries come from your grub.cfg — this only restyles them.
# Tip: set  GRUB_TIMEOUT=5  in /etc/default/grub to match the previewed countdown.

title-text: ""
desktop-image: "background.png"
desktop-image-scale-method: "crop"
desktop-color: "#000000"
message-color: "#3bd16f"
# GRUB shows a terminal for the "Loading Linux ..." messages after you pick an entry. We do NOT
# style it as a box (no terminal-box); instead we make it full-screen, so it's a plain normal
# boot transition (like a text console) rather than the centered green-bordered panel it was.
terminal-left: "0"
terminal-top: "0"
terminal-width: "100%"
terminal-height: "100%"

# pre-rendered title block (Share Tech Mono — GRUB can't load TTF fonts), like the studio preview
+ image { file = "title.png"  left = 33%  top = 21%  width = 34%  height = 12% }

# item_font is the project's real font, shipped as share-tech-16.pf2 — grub2-mkconfig
# emits a loadfont line for every .pf2 in the theme dir (that's why the apply step matters)
+ boot_menu {
  left = 27%  top = 40%  width = 46%  height = 38%
  item_font = "Share Tech Mono Regular 16"
  item_color = "#3bd16f"
  selected_item_color = "#7dff96"
  selected_item_pixmap_style = "sel_*.png"
  item_height = 24
  item_padding = 2
  item_spacing = 14
  item_icon_space = 0
  icon_width = 0
  icon_height = 0
}

+ label { top = 84% left = 0 width = 100% align = "center"
  text = "Use the ↑ and ↓ keys to select · Enter to boot · 'e' to edit · 'c' for a command line"
  color = "#3bd16f" }
+ label { top = 88% left = 0 width = 100% align = "center" id = "__timeout__"
  text = "The highlighted entry will boot automatically in %d s."
  color = "#3bd16f" }
